inline_comments.message: y is this different from the above APIs?
not very sure y do we hav 2 different base endpoints?
are these always fixed? Should these be configurable?
I don't think so Aditya. There are no parameters passed in to this API through the URL.

I'm not sure 'why' it's different to the other API's in Network-cm-proxy. But I implemented it as outlined in the API details on the wiki for spike CPS-390, which outlines it as {ncmp-root}/ncmp-dmi/v1/ch/.

https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-390+Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+DMI+Plugin+REST+Interface#CPS390Spike:DefineandAgreeDMIPluginRESTInterface-DMIInventory,Model&DataSyncAPI

I created another endpoint as the endpoint for the DMI Registration endpoint has 'ncmp-dmi' after the base path but before v1.

Example {ncmp-root}/ncmp-dmi/v1/ch/

Where as the other endpoints within network-cm-proxy don't contain ncmp-dmi before /v1:
/cps-ncmp/api/v1/cm-handles/{cm-handle}/node.

So to test the endpoint within this class, I had to create another endpoint.
Yes, the anchor and dataspace are fixed for the DMI Registration Handle.
it probably time to delete these old API methods now, this has been agreed in meetings with Ericsson and E2E Slicing team

de old methods are being deprecated and the new |API we intensively discussed and represented.
If anything we need to delete the old API methods now...
as per previous comment, NCMP interface is being re-written and I think we should remove old methods now....
no need to make these configurable, same data is embedded in liquibase files and would be very hard to make configurable. Also simply no need this is data NCMP owns...

don't use method names in descriptions, use PO (product Owner) speak / plain English instead
eg. (when) 'cm handles are registered'
I know you didn't make this change but the "Modification" be "Copyright" in this case

I know you didn't make this change but this should be "Modifications Copyright (C)"
Have Added the Deprecation tag for these API's in the ncmproxy.yml file

this should just be given: 'json data'
no need to add too much detail to the commit message

only used in one tets, just just string literals in te test instead

i think these all deprecations do not relate to this task, they should come from some spike or other task so that its easy to find this change in future.
also deprecated date should be here for best practise.
this api is specifically for component-internal audience. for best practise we should not mix api with differenct audience. this is good in terms of publishing apis to target audience.

is 404 possible in case of post
different file should be used for all the api pointing to dmi
description of this controller method missing
looks like any kind of string can reach service impl, without any validation

i think it should support updation too, are you planning to write put method too. is that a possiblity that dmi can send same same cmhandle multiple times with different data , like additional params.

As i see control of id of resouce is given to client for this post request. So imo post and put in this case are equivalent.

as this service reside in ncmp , this desc looks inappropriate.

Registration of new cmhandles

Me, Toine and Niamh had an internal discussion to deprecate these api's instead of removing them completely as they may be refactored in the future, so there is no point in completely removing them. I can add this to the current tasks Jira though?
For the moment, this is just a post request to for the new cm handle registration and to post to the fragment table. We will be working on update/delete as part of a separate task!

Because we will be refactoring all api's with the new endpoint in this module, it makes sense to keep these API endpoints in the same file currently.

Jira story had been created for this but needs to be groomed furter.

CPS-496
Updating cm handles will be part of a different task

I was following the same format as done for the previous dataspace name. But you're right, seeing as these are constants, it is uneseccary to have a separate method dedicated to just getting the String.

I think it is OK to do it in this task, no need for the overhead of a separate task.
We have created a new task to cleanup the deprecated methods during Istanbul timeframe: https://jira.onap.org/browse/CPS-496

This Test case may not be required if the changes related to object mapper are implemented.

Can add values inline in the changeset instead of reading it from the file

changes:
        - insert:
            tableName: 'fragment'
            columns:
              - column:
                  name: id
                  value: 144
              - column:
                  name: xpath
                  value: /dmi-registry
              - column:
                  name: attributes
                  value: '{}'
              - column:
                  name: anchor_id
                  valueComputed: (select id from anchor where name='ncmp-dmi-registry')
              - column:
                  name: dataspace_id
                  valueComputed: (select id from dataspace where name='NCMP-Admin')

private , if u need to set them use @setter annotation
it is recommended to us constructor autowiring

we are creating new PersistenceCMHandleList for each CM Handle and and at the end we will have same number of peristenceCMHandlesList as we have CMhandle.
Is it expected?

we are adding persistenceCmHandle in persistenceCMHandles but we never reset the value of  persistenceCmHandles. So the last persistenceCMHandlesList will have all the cmHandles.
Is it expected?
It represents only one property. And there is another class AdditionalProperty inside the PersistenceCMHandle. Do we need these two separate classes?
PersistenceCMHandle and CMHandle. These two class names are similar and does not tell much what they represent.
Where is it being used? and the .json files inside the resources folder.

As this is personal preference, what do other people think should be done here?

I am open to changing this to include the string value within the test, as opposed to pointing at a file.
This will not be null as long as we receive data in the body of the post request. As this is what is being received from this getter.

We get an error here is if we don't remove the square bracket which wraps the string
Found an unexpected array nested under (org:onap:cps:ncmp?revision=2021-05-20)dmi-registry".
This comes from the mapper.writevalueasstring function above.

There are two potential properties. We use this model for the initial mapping of the properties to their values, whereas the second model is for persisting to the DB. The naming is confusing, the key in both cases is name and value. Value isn't a value in the mapping sense.

CM Handle is the initial model class which handles the data we receive from DMI, whereas the PersistenceCmHandle is what we then map the values received to before we persist the data to our own DB. Me and Toine came to this naming convention. It can be changed on further discussion if you feel it is not appropriate.
This was left over from old files which read the json data. Is now removed 😊

recommended to use constructor autowiring.
Same comment for all the @component
As persistenceCmHandlesList is getting created only outside the loop, we can execute this statement outside the loop.

Instead of persistenceCmHandlesLists if we use persistenceCmHandlesList variable, we don't have to trim the writeObjectDataAsString. 

Suggestion: 

final String writeObjectDataAsString = mapper.writeValueAsString(persistenceCmHandlesList);
 cpsDataService.saveListNodeData(NCMP_DATASPACE_NAME, NCMP_ANCHOR_NAME, "/dmi-registry",
                writeObjectDataAsString);

agree, neither is right or wrong but so far we have put 'large; json definitions in separate files with a meaningful name. I have a slight preference for that and at this stage it is an established pattenr so lets keep using that
We decided to revert these changes and keep this file to keep it consistent with how other data is loaded into the DB

I suggest to use only one. The data conversion from controller model to service model should be handled in the ncmp-rest.

HttpStatus.CREATED is of type String Renu. This is generated through the open api yml files and can be found in NetworkCmProxyApi.java

Done

This can be kept the same. But map now created, so values don't have to be "name" and "value" and can be anything.
This was suggested by Toine from the previous patchset, as to remove more list creations within the service layer class. I left in the getter and setter accidentally, and have no removed them!
so instead of the "properties" OpenAPI keyword we now use 'additionalProperties' keyword which defined a map in open API
since 'additionalProperties' is a 'reserved word' in API we should rename this to cmHandleProperties
if order is changed below I dont think you need this
Not sure why this is removed but I think we should not remove it.

HttpStatus.CREATED is an HTTP status which is expected to be sent in ResponseEntity. But apart from that, we are expected to return a string in response body too.

If it is not expected, wiki and openapi.yml should be updated.
Either we change it to setCmHandles or call new ( which adds only one cmhandle) addCMHandle inside the loop.
As we are replacing the value in additionalProperties, we should name it setAdditionalProperties

I think he suggested to add one cmhandle at a time otherwise setter should be enough. 

If this method replacing the content, then setCmHandles is a better name then addCMHandles. 'Add' gives the impression that we adding to something existing which is not the case in this method.

I also posted a suggestion in the PersistenceCMHandleList patchset 14 based on Toine's comment.

We aren't replacing values here Renu. We use this method to add the mapped key and value to a list of additional properties.
Changed Name to set cm handle. Kept logic the same, as we are nesting a list within another list, so we shouldn't expect to pass the object within this

I checked in the documentation https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/CPS-390+Spike%3A+Define+and+Agree+DMI+Plugin+REST+Interface. 
and it looks like that no response body is expected.

It is not a direct replace as input parameters is a map and additionalProperties type is a List. But logically we are removing all the values in the additionalProperties and adding them from the map. 
As it is a replace, we should name it 'setAdditionalProperties'

Do we really need ObjectMapper here ? ModelMapper should suffice if its just an object conversion..
ok, so just need to update openapi.yml then?
Hey Aditya, this wasn't used previously within NCMP Proxy which is why it was removed. I can add it back in, but I don't see the point in keeping unused objects within these files.
Mentioned in patchset 14 by Toine to keep the name until we have similar methods.

Is there any advantage to using Model Mapper here over Object Mapper? We used Model Mapper, as it was what was previously used when writing the object as a string, before it was suggested in patchset 11 by Renu to use convert value instead

My initial suggestion was not to use convert object to json and then convert to another Object Type. I found the convertValue method and suggested the same.

ModelMapper has been used in cps for similar use case and should be preferred here for being consistent.
